game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
{
  "original_game_name": "Okey Plus",
  "game_name": "Okey Solitaire",
  "genre": "Tile-Matching Puzzle",
  "core_mechanics": "The player aims to form valid sets (e.g., three 7s of different colors, four 10s of different colors) and runs (e.g., red 4, 5, 6) from a hand of 14 tiles. The game is played against 3 AI opponents. The main gameplay loop consists of a player's turn where they first draw a tile (either from a central face-down draw pile or the last discarded tile of the previous player), then rearrange their hand if desired, and finally discard one tile from their hand to maintain 14 tiles. The goal is to be the first to form a hand where all 14 tiles constitute valid sets and runs, and then declare 'Okey' on their turn, using the 15th tile (drawn tile) as the final discard. A special 'Okey' tile is determined at the start of each round and acts as a wild card, alongside two standard Joker tiles. The game tracks the current player, the state of the central draw pile, each player's hand, and each player's discard pile (only the top tile visible). Validating a hand requires checking for complete sets and runs, where each tile must belong to exactly one set or run.",
  "visual_elements": "The game board will feature a central playing area with the main draw pile, surrounded by four player areas (one for the human player at the bottom, and three AI players at top, left, and right). Each player area will contain a 'rack' to display their current hand of tiles and a 'discard pile' for their discarded tiles.

**Game Objects:**
-   **Tiles:** Rectangular shapes (e.g., 50x75 pixels) with rounded corners. Each tile will have a distinct background color for its suit (Red, Black, Blue, Green) and a large number (1-13) or symbol (for Joker) in its center. The 'Okey' tile for the current round will have a special visual border or glow. Tiles in the draw pile will be face-down (grey patterned back). Tiles in discard piles will be face-up.
-   **Player Racks:** Long, narrow rectangular areas (e.g., 700x100 pixels) at the bottom for the player, and smaller versions for AI opponents, where tiles are displayed horizontally with slight overlap.
-   **Central Draw Pile:** A stack of overlapping face-down tiles in the center of the board.
-   **Discard Piles:** Smaller stacks of overlapping face-up tiles, one next to each player's rack.
-   **Okey Indicator:** A prominent display (e.g., a larger tile icon or text) showing which tile is the 'Okey' wild card for the current round.

**UI Elements:**
-   **Score Display:** Top-right corner: 'SCORE: [Current Score]' and 'HIGH SCORE: [High Score]'.
-   **Level Display:** Top-left corner: 'LEVEL: [Current Level Number]'.
-   **Turn Indicator:** A subtle glow or border around the current player's area.
-   **'Declare Okey' Button:** A small button on the player's UI, active when it's their turn and they have a potential winning hand.
-   **Player Avatars/Names:** Simple icons or text labels for 'Player' and 'AI 1', 'AI 2', 'AI 3'.
-   **Level Transition Screen:** Full-screen text for 'LEVEL X COMPLETE!' and 'PREPARING LEVEL Y...'.

**Animations/Visual Effects:**
-   **Tile Drawing:** A smooth sliding animation of a tile from the draw pile (or AI discard pile) to the player's hand.
-   **Tile Discarding:** A smooth sliding animation of a tile from the player's hand to their discard pile.
-   **Tile Selection/Highlight:** A glowing border or slight lift for the currently selected tile in the player's hand or a selected pile.
-   **Winning Hand Highlight:** When Okey is declared, the winning player's valid sets and runs will briefly animate or flash.
-   **Turn Transition:** A fade or pulse effect on the turn indicator as control shifts between players.
-   **Game Start/Level Transition:** A simple fade-in/fade-out for text overlays.

All rendering will be handled using p5.js drawing functions (rect, text, image/color fills). No physics engine (Matter.js) will be used for tile movement; animations will be managed programmatically.",
  "control_scheme": "The game will primarily use keyboard controls to navigate, select, and manipulate tiles, replacing the original drag-and-drop mechanism. A visual highlight indicates the currently selected tile or pile.

-   **ArrowLeft / ArrowRight:** When focus is on the player's hand, these keys navigate the selected tile within the hand. When a tile is 'picked up' (see Space key), these keys can be used to reorder the picked-up tile within the hand.
-   **ArrowUp:** Moves focus to either the central draw pile or the AI's last discard pile (if it's the player's turn to draw). Subsequent presses cycle between these options if both are available.
-   **ArrowDown:** Moves focus back to the player's hand after selecting a draw option, or to select a tile in the hand for manipulation.
-   **Space:**
    -   If a draw pile (central or AI discard) is selected: Draws a tile into the player's hand.
    -   If a tile in the player's hand is selected: 'Picks up' the tile, making it float slightly above the rack and ready for movement or discard. (The previously selected tile is now 'picked up' and moves with the ArrowLeft/Right keys).
    -   If a tile is 'picked up': Places the tile down at its current position in the hand. This is used for rearranging tiles within the hand.
-   **Shift:** No dedicated function, as Space and Arrow keys cover movement and placement.
-   **D:** If a tile is 'picked up' (after pressing Space on a tile), pressing 'D' will discard this tile to the player's discard pile. This completes the discard phase of the player's turn.
-   **Z:** 'Declare Okey'. If the player has a valid winning hand (14 tiles forming sets/runs) after drawing and before discarding, pressing 'Z' will attempt to declare a win. If valid, the round ends; if not, an error message appears, and the player must continue their turn.
-   **W / A / S:** No dedicated function.

Key combinations: After picking up a tile with `Space`, use `ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight` to slide it to a new position in the hand. Press `Space` again to drop it or `D` to discard it.",
  "game_states": "The game flow is managed through distinct states, with clear transitions:

1.  **START_MENU:**
    -   **Description:** Displays game title, 'Play Game' button, 'Instructions' button, and 'High Score'.
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   'Play Game' button click -> `LEVEL_START` (for Level 1).
        -   'Instructions' button click -> `INSTRUCTIONS`.

2.  **INSTRUCTIONS:**
    -   **Description:** Displays detailed rules and control scheme.
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   'Back to Menu' button click -> `START_MENU`.

3.  **LEVEL_START:**
    -   **Description:** A full-screen overlay showing 'LEVEL [Current Level Number]' and 'GET READY!'. Briefly explains level difficulty.
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   After a 3-second delay -> `DEALING`.

4.  **DEALING:**
    -   **Description:** Tiles are shuffled and distributed to all players (animated). The 'Okey' tile is revealed.
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   Once all tiles are dealt and 'Okey' tile is set -> `PLAYER_TURN_DRAW` (for the human player).

5.  **PLAYER_TURN_DRAW:**
    -   **Description:** Player can choose to draw from the central pile or the previous AI's discard pile using `ArrowUp`/`ArrowDown` and `Space`.
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   Player draws a tile -> `PLAYER_TURN_ARRANGE_DISCARD`.

6.  **PLAYER_TURN_ARRANGE_DISCARD:**
    -   **Description:** Player has 15 tiles. They can rearrange their hand (`Space` + `ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight`) and must discard one tile (`Space` then `D`). They can also attempt to 'Declare Okey' (`Z`).
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   Player discards a tile -> `AI_TURN_1`.
        -   Player successfully declares Okey (`Z`) -> `WIN_ANIMATION`.

7.  **AI_TURN_1 / AI_TURN_2 / AI_TURN_3:**
    -   **Description:** Each AI player performs its turn (draws, evaluates hand, discards). A small delay simulates thinking time.
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   AI completes turn -> Next AI_TURN (e.g., `AI_TURN_1` -> `AI_TURN_2`).
        -   AI 3 completes turn -> `PLAYER_TURN_DRAW` (if no one has won yet).
        -   Any AI declares Okey -> `LOSE_ANIMATION`.
        -   Draw pile empties, and no one wins -> `LOSE_ANIMATION` (stalemate).

8.  **WIN_ANIMATION:**
    -   **Description:** Player's winning hand is highlighted, score updates are displayed, celebratory animation.
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   After animation/delay -> `LEVEL_COMPLETE`.

9.  **LOSE_ANIMATION:**
    -   **Description:** The AI's winning hand is highlighted, 'GAME OVER' message, final score.
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   After animation/delay -> `GAME_OVER`.

10. **LEVEL_COMPLETE:**
    -   **Description:** Displays 'LEVEL X COMPLETE!', current round score, total score, and a 'Next Level' button.
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   'Next Level' button click -> `LEVEL_START` (for the next level).

11. **GAME_OVER:**
    -   **Description:** Displays final score, 'GAME OVER' text, and 'Play Again' button. Updates high score if applicable.
    -   **Transitions:**
        -   'Play Again' button click -> `LEVEL_START` (for Level 1).

**State Management:** A global `gameState` variable will be used, updated by specific functions for state transitions. Each state will have its own `draw()` and `keyPressed()` functions or logic within the main p5.js loop.",
  "win_conditions": "The player wins the current round if they are the first to form a valid 14-tile hand (where all 14 tiles are arranged into legal sets or runs) and then successfully 'Declare Okey' by pressing the 'Z' key on their turn, *before* discarding their 15th tile. This declaration must be valid; if the hand is not complete, the declaration fails, and the player must continue their turn and make a discard. A round win leads to score accumulation and progression to the next level.",
  "lose_conditions": "The player loses the current round if:
1. An AI opponent successfully forms a valid 14-tile hand and declares 'Okey' before the human player.
2. The central draw pile runs out of tiles and no player (human or AI) has managed to complete their hand and declare 'Okey'. In this scenario, the round ends in a stalemate, which is considered a loss for the player for progression purposes.",
  "scoring_system": "A persistent scoring system tracks the player's total score across levels, with a local high-score record.

-   **Round Win Bonus:** +500 points awarded for successfully completing a valid 14-tile hand and declaring Okey.
-   **Tile-Based Bonus:** +50 points for each tile in the *winning hand* that is part of a valid set or run and is *not* a wildcard (Joker or Okey tile). This rewards efficient hand formation.
-   **Opponent Bonus:** +100 points for each AI opponent the player beats in the round (up to +300 points if all three AI opponents are beaten by the player's win).
-   **Level Completion Bonus:** +1000 points awarded upon successfully completing a level (winning the round). This is added to the round score.
-   **Score Multiplier:** The total points earned in a round (Round Win + Tile-Based + Opponent Bonus) will be multiplied by `(1 + (Current Level Number - 1) * 0.1)`. So, Level 1: x1.0, Level 2: x1.1, Level 3: x1.2.

**High Score System:** The highest total score achieved across all completed levels will be saved locally using `localStorage` and displayed on the `START_MENU` screen and `GAME_OVER` screen.

**Score Display:**
-   The current cumulative score for the entire game session will be displayed in the **top-right corner** of the screen during gameplay, formatted as: `SCORE: 12345`.
-   The high score will be shown on the `START_MENU` and `GAME_OVER` screens.

**Score Impact on Gameplay:** The total score is primarily for player achievement and high score tracking. It doesn't directly affect mechanics but provides motivation for progression and better play.",
  "level_system": "The game features 3 distinct levels, each increasing in difficulty by adjusting AI strategy and starting hand favorability.

**Level Indicator:** The current level number will be displayed in the **top-left corner** of the screen during gameplay, formatted as: `LEVEL: 1`.

**Level Transition Display:** Upon completing a level, a full-screen overlay will appear, displaying `LEVEL X COMPLETE!` and listing the score for that round. After a brief pause, it will transition to `PREPARING LEVEL Y...` before the next level begins.

1.  **Level 1: The Apprentice**
    -   **Difficulty:** Easiest.
    -   **AI Strategy:** Basic. AI players prioritize discarding the highest value non-matching tile. They mostly draw from the central pile, only taking an opponent's discard if it forms an immediate, obvious set or run (e.g., completes a pair into a triplet, or extends a run by one tile).
    -   **Player Hand Advantage:** The player's initial hand is slightly 'lucky', often containing a few pre-formed pairs or the start of a run, making it easier to form a winning hand.
    -   **Advancement:** Win the round -> Advance to Level 2.

2.  **Level 2: The Journeyman**
    -   **Difficulty:** Medium.
    -   **AI Strategy:** Improved. AI players now evaluate their hand more effectively, trying to complete multiple potential sets/runs. They are more strategic about discarding, avoiding giving opponents obvious useful tiles if possible. They will also consider drawing from previous discards more often if it benefits their hand significantly.
    -   **Player Hand:** Starting hands are randomized, similar to standard play, removing the slight advantage from Level 1.
    -   **Advancement:** Win the round -> Advance to Level 3.

3.  **Level 3: The Master**
    -   **Difficulty:** Hardest.
    -   **AI Strategy:** Advanced. AI players are more aggressive in completing their Okey. They employ basic 'tile counting' heuristics, remembering which valuable tiles have been discarded or are likely still in the draw pile. They are highly efficient at forming sets/runs and will quickly declare Okey if they achieve a winning hand. They will actively try to block opponents by not discarding tiles that could be useful.
    -   **Player Hand:** Fully randomized, no special considerations.
    -   **Advancement:** Win the round -> Game Completed (returns to `GAME_OVER` screen with final score).

**Progression:** The player advances to the next level automatically upon winning the current round. There is no option to skip levels or choose difficulty.",
  "simplifications": "To adapt Okey Plus for an HTML5 single-player experience using p5.js and meeting the specified requirements, several key simplifications have been made:

1.  **Single-Player Conversion:** The original online multiplayer aspect is entirely removed. The game is played by one human player against three AI opponents. This eliminates the need for network code, lobbies, real-time communication, and player synchronization.
2.  **Chip Economy and Game Rooms:** The in-game chip currency, 'higher-stakes game rooms,' and daily login bonuses are removed. Game progression is now purely driven by winning rounds and advancing through a fixed set of difficulty levels, with points replacing chips as a measure of success.
3.  **Global Leaderboards:** Replaced by a local high-score system stored in the browser's `localStorage`.
4.  **Control Scheme Conversion:** The original 'drag and drop' touch/mouse interaction is replaced with a keyboard-centric control scheme (using Arrow keys, Space, and D) as per the prompt's explicit requirement. This significantly alters the user interaction model but ensures keyboard accessibility.
5.  **AI Complexity:** The AI opponents use simplified heuristic-based logic for drawing, hand evaluation, and discarding. They do not employ complex machine learning or deep strategic analysis, focusing instead on predefined rules for forming sets/runs and minimizing discards useful to the player.
6.  **Full Okey Rule Set:** While the core rules for sets, runs, Jokers, and the special 'Okey' tile are retained, any highly nuanced or less common rules (e.g., specific scoring for specific winning hands beyond basic completion, complex discard penalties, or multiple Okey types) are abstracted or omitted for simplicity and focus on the core winning condition.",
  "implementation_notes": "The game will be implemented using p5.js for all graphical rendering, input handling, and game logic. Matter.js will not be utilized as the tile-matching nature of Okey does not necessitate a physics engine; all tile movements and visual effects will be handled programmatically with p5.js animation techniques. 

**Score Display:** The `SCORE: [current_score]` will be dynamically updated and rendered in the top-right corner of the canvas using `text()` functions. The `HIGH SCORE: [high_score]` will be similarly displayed on the start menu and game over screens, loaded from/saved to `localStorage`.

**Level Progression:** Levels are linear. A player must complete Level 1 to unlock Level 2, and so on. There is no branching or skipping of levels. Upon successful completion of Level 3, the game concludes, and the player is shown the `GAME_OVER` screen. Level information (`LEVEL: [current_level]`) will be consistently displayed in the top-left corner during gameplay."
}
game_info.description: Welcome to Okey Solitaire! Your goal is to arrange your 14 tiles into valid sets (3 or 4 tiles of the same number in different colors) and runs (3+ consecutive numbered tiles of the same color). Compete against 3 AI opponents to be the first to complete a winning hand and declare "Okey". Each turn, draw a tile from the center pile or an opponent's discard, then rearrange your hand and discard one tile. A special Okey tile acts as a wild card. Win rounds to progress through 3 increasingly challenging levels!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left/Right: Navigate tiles in your hand or move a picked-up tile
Arrow Up: Focus on draw piles (center or opponent's discard)
Arrow Down: Return focus to your hand
Space: Draw from selected pile, pick up/place down a tile in hand
D: Discard the currently picked-up tile
Z: Declare Okey (attempt to win the round)
Enter: Start game from menu
Esc: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart and return to menu
